symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic activity-related weight-bearing joint pain, relieved by rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute mechanical flare of severe knee osteoarthritis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical, load-related pain relieved by rest is characteristic of osteoarthritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Short morning stiffness resolving within 30 minutes

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute mechanical flare of severe knee osteoarthritis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Brief morning stiffness (<30 min) favours OA over an inflammatory arthritis with prolonged stiffness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Coarse crepitus / grinding on joint movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute mechanical flare of severe knee osteoarthritis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable/audible crepitus reflects degenerate cartilage and osteophytes.
Inability to weight-bear on an acute monoarthritis is a red flag that mandates septic-joint exclusion.
Sudden non-weight-bearing in an osteoporosis-risk elder can signal an occult fracture.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, rigors or systemic toxicity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic features lowers septic probability but does not exclude it in a frail elder.

symptoms.symptom_name: No prior episode of a hot, red, acutely swollen joint

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No prior explosive hot-joint episodes makes recurrent crystal disease less likely, though a first presentation remains possible.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild cool joint effusion without warmth, erythema or exquisite tenderness

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute mechanical flare of severe knee osteoarthritis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A cool, mildly effused joint argues strongly against septic arthritis — but this is a clinician hands-on finding, sealed and unobtainable via telehealth.
Absence of warmth/erythema on examination lowers septic probability; requires in-person joint examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heberden and Bouchard nodes; quadriceps disuse atrophy

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Generalised osteoarthritis (bilateral knees and right hip, Heberden/Bouchard nodes)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bony DIP/PIP enlargements are hallmark generalised OA signs; clinician examination finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severely restricted painful range of motion (flexion limited to ~40 degrees)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute mechanical flare of severe knee osteoarthritis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marked restriction quantifies the flare severity; requires hands-on examination, unobtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Plain radiograph: joint-space narrowing with marginal osteophytes (anticipated)

investigation_details.expected_result: Joint-space narrowing, subchondral sclerosis and marginal osteophytes consistent with severe osteoarthritis; excludes an obvious fracture
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An occult fracture or the degree of degenerative change is not characterised.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute mechanical flare of severe knee osteoarthritis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Radiographic OA tetrad confirms the degenerative substrate; imaging is a live-data result never obtainable in real-time telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person joint examination (warmth, erythema, effusion, range of motion) ± joint aspiration
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only hands-on assessment ± aspiration can exclude septic arthritis and crystal arthropathy in an acute monoarthritis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Inflammatory bloods (FBE, CRP, ESR, urate) and blood cultures if any systemic features
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support septic-joint exclusion; unobtainable via telehealth and required same-day if red flags emerge.
Characterises degenerative change and excludes an occult periarticular/insufficiency fracture in a non-weight-bearing elder.
